title: carlina gorterioides
intro: Carlina gorterioides, also known as alpine hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its yellow flowers and prickly leaves. Traditionally, Carlina gorterioides has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which make it effective in treating various health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to its health benefits. Carlina gorterioides is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it has also been used in traditional folk medicine to treat digestive issues and promote wound healing.
